name: Natalie Cole
abstract: Natalie Maria Cole (February 6, 1950 – December 31, 2015) was an American singer, songwriter, and actress. She was the daughter of singer and jazz pianist Nat King Cole.

concerts.label: Lauri Strauss Leukemia Foundation Benefit Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: They jumped up on the charts, above Natalie Cole’s Unforgettable, when they started.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Mancuso
about_quotes.evidence: It’s just like Natalie Cole. You want to do what your father did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Mancuso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 10, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been traveling with Natalie Cole for the last six and a half years. In fact we just got back from the east coast with her a couple of weeks ago.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been doing nothing but large orchestra things for the last five years for people... in making records with Natalie Cole
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: I got to be on the road with Natalie Cole with the “Unforgettable” album and, as her pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Nat Cole (Natalie's father) extensively, mentioning recording his tunes and touring with his band, but makes no mention of meeting or interacting with Natalie Cole herself, only referencing her album 'Unforgettable'.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]
